title: Crispy Egyptian Orange Tart
description: A light, citrusy tart with a flaky Egyptian pastry crust, silky orange custard, and a caramelized sugar finish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and grease a 23 cm (9 inch) tart tin.
In a bowl, combine flour, icing sugar, and a pinch of salt. Add the cold butter cubes and rub them into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add the egg yolk and cold water; mix until a dough forms. Wrap in cling film and chill for 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough on a lightly floured surface to fit the tart tin; line the crust and trim the edges.
Blind bake the crust for 15 minutes, then remove and set aside.
In a saucepan, whisk together 200 g sugar, eggs, cornstarch, orange juice, and orange zest until smooth.
Heat gently, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon (about 5 minutes).
Remove from heat, stir in 50 g butter and orange liqueur (if using) until melted and glossy.
Pour the custard into the pre-baked crust, smoothing the top.
Bake the tart for 20 minutes, or until the custard sets and the edges are golden.
Meanwhile, melt 50 g sugar, 2 tbsp butter, and 2 tbsp water in a small pan over medium heat, stirring until it turns golden brown. Drizzle this caramelized syrup over the warm tart.
Allow the tart to cool slightly before slicing and serving.
